meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve no portfolio (Advanced Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no portfolio. A Advanced-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Starter Kits to Fix No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no portfolio' trap by building a high-end SaaS starter kit. Learn how to use Antigravity and Claude Code to ship professional apps in hours.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap is solved by building a modular SaaS starter kit that demonstrates architectural mastery. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for automated implementation, you can produce production-grade, highly scalable SaaS foundations that prove your technical capability to high-paying clients instantly.
content.intro:
The silence of a blank portfolio is deafening. You have the skills, the ambition, and the vision, but prospective clients demand proof of work before they trust you with their capital. Chasing one-off gigs to build a track record is a slow death for a high-level developer; you need a force multiplier that showcases engineering excellence without the six-month development cycles.

Enter the SaaS starter kit—a hyper-optimized, reusable foundation that functions as both your product and your proof. By treating your own infrastructure as a high-value asset, you stop selling 'hours' and start selling 'solutions.' It is time to replace your lack of history with an undeniable display of architectural sophistication.

steps.title: Define the Atomic Architecture
steps.description: Establish the foundational stack—Next.js, Supabase, and Stripe—as your core framework to ensure rapid, repeatable deployments.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a production-ready SaaS architecture using Next.js 14, App Router, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS. Implement a modular structure that separates core auth, database schema, and payment webhooks.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate a project structure for a scalable SaaS starter kit including Drizzle ORM and Supabase integration.'

steps.title: Implement Robust Authentication
steps.description: Handle the heavy lifting of user sessions and protected routes to ensure your starter kit is immediately enterprise-ready.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a secure authentication middleware for Next.js that handles session persistence and role-based access control (RBAC) using Supabase Auth.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --execute 'npx supabase init && npx supabase login'

steps.title: Integrate Global Billing Logic
steps.description: Abstract the Stripe checkout flow into a reusable service layer, making it trivial to swap in for future client projects.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Develop a modular Stripe billing handler that supports subscription tiers, webhooks for event handling, and a clean customer portal integration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Create a Stripe webhook handler service that updates user subscription status in the database.'

steps.title: Design the UI Component Library
steps.description: Build a set of atomic UI components that allow you to spin up a professional dashboard for any client in under an hour.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a set of Shadcn/UI components tailored for a SaaS dashboard, including data tables, sidebar navigation, and card-based analytics layouts.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --execute 'npx shadcn@latest add table card button sidebar'

steps.title: Automate Deployment Pipelines
steps.description: Configure Vercel or GitHub Actions to ensure that every push to the repo triggers a successful, error-free production build.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a CI/CD pipeline for the starter kit that runs linting, type-checking, and unit tests before deployment to Vercel.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate a .github/workflows/deploy.yml file for automated testing and production deployment.'

pro_tips: Always build your SaaS starter kit with a 'plugin' architecture so you can swap out databases or auth providers without rewriting the core business logic.
Use Claude Code to document your code as you write it; clients pay 20% more for clean, well-documented codebases.
Include a 'Demo Mode' in your starter kit that uses seeded mock data to impress clients during live video calls.
Prioritize 'Observability'—integrate logging early so you can prove to clients that your apps are stable and monitorable.
Never hardcode environment variables; use a strictly typed configuration utility to ensure your starter kit is bulletproof.
content.next_steps: Deploy your finished starter kit to a public GitHub repository, write a compelling README, and start reaching out to founders with a 'build-ready' solution rather than a cold pitch.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for the 'perfect' project and start building the foundation of your freelance empire today. Your portfolio is not a record of the past, but a promise of future speed. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
